meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 10)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Final review: master singular and plural noun rules with challenging sentences.

questions.question: What is the plural of "ant"?
questions.explanation: ant → ants. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: "Houses" is the plural of "house".
questions.explanation: Yes! house → houses. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bird".
questions.explanation: bird → birds. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: There are many ___ in the city. (church)
questions.explanation: church → churches (add -es). "many" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: The plural of "star" is "stars".
questions.explanation: Yes! star → stars. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "box".
questions.explanation: box → boxes. Words ending in -x add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence uses the correct plural noun?
options: I plant two bush in the yard.
I plant two bushs in the yard.
I plant two bushes in the yard.
I plant two bushies in the yard.
questions.explanation: bush → bushes (add -es). "two" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: ant/bird/star = one (singular). houses/churches = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Roy has two glasses of juice."

questions.question: The plural of "pencil" is "pencils".
questions.explanation: Yes! pencil → pencils. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "beach".
questions.explanation: beach → beaches. Words ending in -ch add -es.

questions.question: Which pair shows the correct singular → plural change?
questions.explanation: box → boxes is correct. box ends in -x, so add -es.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -x or -ch add -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "The class has twenty students," the word "class" is a singular noun.
questions.explanation: "The class" (one class) — that's singular. "classes" would be the plural.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "We see many churches in the city." (church → churches)

questions.question: Write the plural of "ruler".
questions.explanation: ruler → rulers. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
questions.explanation: car → cars. "five" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: A noun ending in -ch will become plural by adding ___.
questions.explanation: Nouns ending in -ch add -es to form the plural (e.g., beach → beaches, church → churches).

questions.question: The sentence "Some bears eat fish" uses the correct plural form.
questions.explanation: bears is the correct plural of bear (add -s). "Some" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bus".
questions.explanation: bus → buses. Words ending in -s add -es.
